metadata.name: Core Audit Rules
metadata.description: Basic SEO and agent-readiness checks for any website.

SEO-01.name: Single H1 Per Page
SEO-01.description: Each page must have exactly one H1 tag. Multiple H1s confuse AI agents and search engines about the primary topic. Zero H1s make the page untitled for agents.

fix.instruction: Ensure every page has exactly one <h1> tag describing the primary topic. If multiple H1s exist, downgrade extras to <h2>. If zero exist, add one at the top of <body>.
fix.codeSnippet: Primary Topic of This Page

SEO-02.name: Title Tag Presence and Length
SEO-02.description: Every page must have a <title> tag between 30 and 60 characters. Missing titles make pages untitled in search results and agent context. Too long gets truncated.

fix.instruction: Add a <title> tag inside <head> with 30-60 characters. Include primary keyword and brand name.

AG-01.name: HTML Lang Attribute
AG-01.description: The <html> tag must have a lang attribute. AI agents use this to determine language for summarization, translation, and content processing. Missing lang causes agent misinterpretation.

fix.instruction: Add lang attribute to the <html> tag using ISO 639-1 language code.
fix.codeSnippet:
<html lang="en">
<!-- or lang="bn" for Bangla, lang="zh" for Chinese -->

SEO-03.name: Meta Description Presence
SEO-03.description: Each page should have a meta description between 120 and 160 characters. While not a ranking factor, it controls search snippet text and agent summary context.

fix.instruction: Add a meta description summarizing the page content in 120-160 characters.
fix.codeSnippet: <meta name="description" content="This page covers backend engineering with NestJS, PostgreSQL, and scalable API design for high-traffic systems.">

examples.input: <meta name="description" content="A comprehensive guide to building scalable backends with NestJS and PostgreSQL for modern applications.">

SEO-04.name: Canonical Link Tag
SEO-04.description: Each page should have a canonical URL to prevent duplicate content issues. AI agents use canonicals to identify the authoritative version of content.

fix.instruction: Add a canonical link tag pointing to the preferred version of this URL.
